<!-- Generated by ensemble-pi generator v1.0.0 | Source: check-feature-drift.yaml -->
<!-- Command: ensemble:check-feature-drift | Version: 1.0.0 -->
<!-- Description: Detect drift between a PRD's acceptance criteria and its generated Gherkin feature files -->

# ensemble:check-feature-drift

> **Mission:** Re-parse a PRD and compare its current acceptance criteria against the .prd-trace.json manifest produced by /ensemble:generate-feature-tests. Reports ADDED acceptance criteria with no feature scenario, REMOVED criteria whose feature scenario is now orphaned, and CHANGED criteria whose wording has materially diverged from the generated feature. This makes requirement/test traceability continuous rather than a one-time snapshot, and the CLI exit code (0 in-sync, 2 drift) lets the same check run as a CI or pre-commit gate.

> **Constraints:**
> - This command is READ-ONLY -- it never rewrites feature files; it reports drift and tells the user how to resync
> - Comparison is deterministic via the prd-cli helper against the .prd-trace.json manifest -- DO NOT eyeball-diff files
> - Drift identity is the AC id; the fingerprint ignores cosmetic edits (whitespace/casing) so only semantic changes register

## Phase 1: Check

### Step 1: Resolve the PRD path

Determine which PRD to check

**Actions:**
1. If the user passed a PRD path argument, use it
2. Otherwise look under docs/PRD/ and ask the user which PRD to check if ambiguous

### Step 2: Run the drift check

Invoke the deterministic CLI to diff the PRD against its manifest

**Actions:**
1. Resolve PRD_CLI to first existing path among: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/lib/prd-cli.js, packages/product/lib/prd-cli.js. If missing, print error and HALT.
2. Run: node "$PRD_CLI" check-drift <prd-path> --json
3. Pass through --out <dir> (default: features/) so the correct manifest is located
4. Interpret exit code: 0 = IN_SYNC, 2 = drift detected, 1 = no manifest yet

### Step 3: Report and recommend

Explain the drift and the path back to sync

**Actions:**
1. If IN_SYNC: confirm the feature files match the PRD
2. If ADDED/REMOVED/CHANGED: list the affected AC ids under each category
3. If no manifest exists: tell the user to run /ensemble:generate-feature-tests first
4. Recommend re-running /ensemble:generate-feature-tests to resync, then committing the updated features and manifest
